# WorldSpec

> WorldSpec is a declarative language and compiler for modeling software systems — in any language or product — as entities, relationships, states, invariants, actions, and transitions, so that proposed changes can be checked against critical invariants before they are executed. It is technology-agnostic and imposes no domain vocabulary of its own.

A WorldSpec model is a directory of YAML documents using six construct kinds
(entity, relationship, state, invariant, action, transition). The compiler
validates a model (stable WS-SYN-* / WS-SEM-* diagnostics; no `eval`) and emits
a neutral canonical JSON IR. To generate WorldSpec from an LLM, give the model
the authoring prompt and the JSON Schema below, then validate the output.

## Specification
- [Language Specification v0.1](https://w3id.org/worldspec/spec/0.1/): normative syntax, type system, expression/action grammars (EBNF), canonical IR, and conformance
- [Namespace 0.1](https://w3id.org/worldspec/ns/0.1/): the reserved terms (construct keywords, type vocabulary, controlled value sets)
- [Model JSON Schema (0.1)](https://w3id.org/worldspec/schemas/worldspec-model-0.1.schema.json): machine-readable structural schema for a model document

## Guides
- [What is WorldSpec?](https://w3id.org/worldspec/what-is-worldspec): the language in five minutes, with a complete example model
- [Authoring with an LLM](https://w3id.org/worldspec/authoring-with-an-llm): a paste-ready system prompt for generating conforming WorldSpec with any model
- [Authoring guide](https://w3id.org/worldspec/usage): turn a real codebase (any stack) into a validated model

## Examples
- [COBOL -> Java cutover](https://w3id.org/worldspec/demo-scenario): application-modernization estate and the invariants a cutover must preserve
- [Short-interest rebate (securities lending)](https://w3id.org/worldspec/example-short-interest-rebate): a broker-dealer estate with collateralization and rebate invariants

## Optional
- [Full single-file language reference](https://w3id.org/worldspec/llms-full.txt): the whole language + schema + a worked example in one file, for pasting into a context window
- [Governance and versioning](https://github.com/banuap/worldspec/blob/main/GOVERNANCE.md)
- [Conformance suite](https://github.com/banuap/worldspec/tree/main/conformance)
- [Reference implementation](https://github.com/banuap/worldspec)
